summary: AWS WAF & Secrets Manager (Phase D) integration testing complete. All backend APIs pass (16/16 - 100%). Frontend page fully functional with WAF and Secrets Manager tabs.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: Backend routes for secrets needed ':path' type annotation to handle secret names with slashes (e.g., 'bigmann/development/web3'). Changed @router.get('/secrets/{secret_name}') to @router.get('/secrets/{secret_name:path}')
Fixed: AWSWafSecretsPage.jsx was using 'const { token } = useAuth()' but AuthContext doesn't expose 'token'. Changed to localStorage.getItem('token') and removed unused useAuth import
seed_data_creation: Test Web ACLs, IP Sets, and Secrets created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: AWS WAF & Secrets Manager Phase D is implemented and working. Both AWS services connect to real AWS in us-east-1 region. Frontend accessible at /aws-security via Tools dropdown > 'Security (WAF)'. WAF shows 15 AWS managed rule groups, and Secrets Manager shows 8+ existing secrets (bigmann/development/*). Secret names contain slashes - backend routes now use :path type to handle this correctly.

features_tested.GET /api/aws-security/status: PASSED - Returns available:true for both WAF and Secrets Manager
features_tested.GET /api/aws-security/waf/web-acls: PASSED - Returns Web ACLs list (currently 0 custom ACLs)
features_tested.GET /api/aws-security/waf/managed-rules: PASSED - Returns 15 AWS managed rule groups
features_tested.Authentication required endpoints: PASSED - All endpoints correctly return 401/403 without auth
features_tested.Frontend /aws-security page: PASSED - Page loads with title 'AWS Security' and status cards
features_tested.Frontend WAF tab: PASSED - Shows Web ACLs, IP Sets, and 15 Managed Rules sections
features_tested.Frontend Secrets tab: PASSED - Shows 9 secrets with create/delete/details actions
features_tested.Frontend navigation: PASSED - 'Security (WAF)' link in Tools dropdown navigates to /aws-security

bugs_fixed_during_testing.issue: GET /api/aws-security/secrets/{secret_name} returned 404 for secrets with slashes in name
bugs_fixed_during_testing.root_cause: FastAPI path parameter didn't handle slashes. Secret names like 'bigmann/development/web3' were parsed incorrectly

bugs_fixed_during_testing.issue: Frontend page failed to load data due to missing auth token
bugs_fixed_during_testing.root_cause: Page used 'const { token } = useAuth()' but AuthContext doesn't expose token
bugs_fixed_during_testing.fix: Changed to 'const token = localStorage.getItem('token')' and removed unused useAuth import
